question: What are the pass marks for the Project Demonstration Oral and written Dissertation element in the MSc Applications of Artificial Intelligence programme?
expected_answer: Students must achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and the written Dissertation element to pass the module and achieve the credits.
follow_up_question: Are there any specific conditions or requirements for passing the MSc Applications of Artificial Intelligence programme other than the pass marks in the Project Demonstration Oral and written Dissertation element?
follow_up_expected_answer: No, the only requirement stated is that students must achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and the written Dissertation element to pass the module and achieve the credits.

question: What is the pass mark for the MSc Applications of Artificial Intelligence programme in the Project Demonstration Oral and written Dissertation element?
expected_answer: Students must achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral and the written Dissertation element to pass the module.
follow_up_question: What is the minimum requirement for passing the MSc Financial Technology (Computer Science) programme in terms of the Project Demonstration Oral and written Dissertation element?
follow_up_expected_answer: Students must achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral and the written Dissertation element to pass the module.

question: What is the pass mark for the MSc Artificial Intelligence in Finance programme on the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and written Dissertation element?
expected_answer: Students are required to achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and the written Dissertation element.
follow_up_question: What is the requirement for passing the MSc Dissertation module?
follow_up_expected_answer: Students must achieve a pass mark of 50 in both the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and the written Dissertation element.

question: What is the pass mark for core modules on the MSc Applications of Artificial Intelligence programme?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules, including core modules, is 50.
follow_up_question: What criteria must be met to condone a failure in up to 20 credits of non-core taught modules on the MSc Applications of Artificial Intelligence programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: A student may condone a failure with a mark of 40 or more for up to 20 credits in non-core taught modules if the overall weighted average for all taught modules is 50. The student must still achieve a minimum mark of 40 in both the coursework aggregate and the exam mark for failure in the module.
follow_up_keyphrases: failure with a mark of 40 or more

question: What is the pass mark for all modules on the MSc Artificial Intelligence (T/F) programme at CSEE?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
follow_up_question: What condition must be met in both coursework and exam marks to round up a module aggregate mark of 39.5 – 39.9 to 40?
follow_up_expected_answer: Marks of 39.5 – 39.9 will be rounded up to 40 if the student achieves a minimum mark of 40 in both the coursework aggregate mark and the exam mark.

question: What is the pass mark for the MSc Artificial Intelligence and its Applications programme in the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and written Dissertation element?
expected_answer: Students are required to achieve a pass mark of 50 in the Project Demonstration Oral (PDO) and 50 in the written Dissertation element.
follow_up_question: What is the review frequency for these Rules of Assessment?
follow_up_expected_answer: The review frequency is annually.

question: What is the pass mark for core modules on the MFA Theatre Directing programme?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree, including core modules, is 50.
follow_up_question: How many credits must be passed and what are the criteria for condoning failures in non-core taught modules on the MFA Theatre Directing programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: To pass, a student should attempt 300 credits at Masters Level (Level 7) of which 260 must be passed with the remaining credits condoned. Failure with a mark of 40 or more for up to 40 credits in non-core taught modules may be condoned provided the overall weighted average for all the taught modules is 50.

question: What is the pass mark for all modules of the MSc Human Resource Management (Full and Part-Time) programme?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
follow_up_question: How many credits must be passed, with the remaining condoned, to achieve a pass in the MSc Human Resource Management (Full and Part-Time) programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: To be awarded a pass in the Masters, a student should attempt 180 credits at Masters Level (Level 7), of which 140 must be passed, with the remaining credits condoned.

question: How long do students have to complete their MBA studies according to the University of Essex's rules for Masters of Business Administration Degrees by Modular Study 2025-26?
expected_answer: Students must complete their studies within 5 years.
follow_up_question: What are the accrediting bodies responsible for setting the rules on study completion time for MBA students at University of Essex?
follow_up_expected_answer: The rules are set by accrediting bodies.

question: What is the maximum period within which students must complete their Masters of Business Administration Degrees by Modular Study according to University of Essex's rules?
expected_answer: Students must complete their studies within 5 years.
follow_up_question: Who approved the rules for the Masters of Business Administration Degrees by Modular Study at University of Essex?
follow_up_expected_answer: The Senate approved the rules.

question: What are the minimum overall weighted average marks required for Pass with Merit and Distinction on the MRes Degree in the Department of Government?
expected_answer: A Pass with Merit requires an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more, while a Pass with Distinction requires an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.
keyphrases: overall weighted average mark of 60 or more
overall weighted average mark of 70 or more
follow_up_question: How many credits must be passed at Masters Level for a student to achieve a Pass on the MRes Degree in the Department of Government?
follow_up_expected_answer: To achieve a pass, students should attempt 360 credits at Masters Level (Level 7), of which 300 must be passed, with the remaining credits condoned.

question: What is the pass mark for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on the MSc Speech and Language Therapy (Pre-registration) programme?
expected_answer: The student must achieve a pass mark of at least 74.
follow_up_question: What is the formula used to adjust the grade for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on this programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: Transformed grade = 1.92 x [raw grade – 74] + 50.

question: What is the pass mark for all modules in the MSc Occupational Therapy (Pre-registration) programme at the University of Essex?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
follow_up_question: What are the criteria for a student to be awarded a Distinction in the MSc Occupational Therapy (Pre-registration) programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: To obtain a Distinction, in addition to achieving the requirements for a pass, a student should have an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.

question: What is the pass mark for all modules on the MSc Advanced Clinical Practice (ACP)/PG Dip/PG Cert Health Care Practice programme at the University of Essex?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules on this programme is 50.
follow_up_question: What are the progression requirements for students in the MSc Advanced Clinical Practitioner Apprenticeship pathway at the University of Essex?
follow_up_expected_answer: Progression is based on passing all assessments and all modules, with students required to pass all individual components of assessment.

question: What is the minimum pass mark for core modules on the PG Cert Psychological Wellbeing Practice and PG Cert Specialist Adult Mental Health (Mental Health Wellbeing Practitioner) programmes?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all components, including core modules, is 50.
follow_up_question: What is the process for reassessment of failed taught modules in these programmes?
follow_up_expected_answer: Students who have failed any taught module may have a second attempt at the assessment for the module(s); however, only one opportunity to resubmit the failed component will be offered.

question: What is the pass mark for all modules of the Graduate Certificate: Psychological Wellbeing Practice and Graduate Certificate: Specialist Adult Mental Health (Mental Health Wellbeing Practitioner) programme?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Graduate Certificate is 40.
follow_up_question: What will be the module aggregate if a student's pre-resubmission module aggregate was above the pass mark and they resubmit their work?
follow_up_expected_answer: The module aggregate is capped at the pre-resubmission aggregate.

question: What is the pass mark for core modules on the Postgraduate Diploma Periodontology programme?
expected_answer: A student must pass all taught core modules with no condonement of marks below a pass allowed.
follow_up_question: What is the overall weighted average mark required for a student to obtain a Merit in the Postgraduate Diploma Periodontology programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: To obtain a Merit, a student should have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more.

question: What is the pass mark for all modules of the MSc Periodontology and MSc Advanced Periodontal Practice programmes?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
follow_up_question: What happens if a student fails an assignment on second submission during in-year Board of Examiners meetings?
follow_up_expected_answer: An in-year Board of Examiners meeting may be convened, and the Board will either withdraw the student or offer additional assessment opportunities such as reassessment for uncapped module marks or voluntary reassessment.

question: What is the minimum pass mark required for all modules on the MA Social Work programme at the University of Essex?
expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
follow_up_question: What criteria must be met to obtain a Merit in the MA Social Work programme at the University of Essex?
follow_up_expected_answer: To obtain a Merit, a student should have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more.

question: What is the pass mark for modules in Year Four of the MSci Nursing programme at Health and Social Care?
expected_answer: The pass mark for modules in Year Four is 50.
follow_up_question: What happens if a student fails an assessment, including a practice-based assessment, on the final attempt in Year Four of the MSci Nursing programme?
follow_up_expected_answer: If a student fails an assessment on the final attempt they will be required to withdraw from the programme except in the case of valid Extenuating Circumstances.
